candidates.name: Deb Fischer
candidates.notes: Senior Senator from Nebraska (2013-). Presbyterian. Cattle rancher. Heritage Action lifetime 70%. NRA A — endorsed and described as 'vocal champion of 2A freedoms.' First woman elected to full Senate term from Nebraska. Pro-life, strong defense hawk.

profile.background: Cattle rancher and former Nebraska State Senator. Runs a ranch in Valentine, Nebraska with her husband. Served in the Nebraska Unicameral from 2005-2013.

aipac.note: TrackAIPAC career-total report shows $632,220 from AIPAC and pro-Israel-lobby PACs.

claims.text: Holds an A+ rating from SBA Pro-Life America and was endorsed by SBA Pro-Life America Candidate Fund for her successful 2024 re-election; supports defunding Planned Parenthood and opposes taxpayer-funded abortion.

Was among 31 Senate Republicans who voted against the Fiscal Responsibility Act of 2023, the bipartisan debt-ceiling compromise, viewing it as insufficiently stringent on federal spending and deficit reduction.
Voted against the Respect for Marriage Act (2022); she was not among the 12 Senate Republicans who supported the bill's passage, holding to the traditional definition of marriage.

candidates.name: Pete Ricketts
candidates.notes: Junior Senator from Nebraska (2023-). Roman Catholic — member Knights of Columbus and Knight of the Holy Sepulchre. Heritage Action lifetime 73%. NRA A. Former 40th Governor (2015-2023). Son of TD Ameritrade founder. Signed Religious Liberty Day proclamation. Draws on Catholic faith for anti-abortion, anti-gay-marriage positions. Pro-death penalty despite Catholic Church opposition.

profile.education: University of Chicago (BA Biology, MBA Marketing/Finance)
profile.background: Former Governor of Nebraska and Ameritrade executive. Son of TD Ameritrade founder Joe Ricketts. Co-owner of Chicago Cubs. Appointed to Senate in 2023 to replace Ben Sasse.

claims.text: Supports a total abortion ban with no exceptions for rape or incest. Following the 2023 Nebraska March for Life, co-sponsored the No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion Act, the Child Interstate Abortion Notification Act, and the SAVE Moms and Babies Act. In 2024 personally donated $500,000 to defeat a pro-abortion constitutional amendment in Nebraska — affirming that life must be protected from conception.

As governor, declared Nebraska a Second Amendment 'sanctuary state,' pledging that state resources would not enforce unconstitutional federal gun restrictions. As senator, co-signed a 2024 amicus curiae brief urging the U.S. Supreme Court to reject Biden administration firearm regulations and protect gun owners' rights — opposing new bans, registries, and magazine restrictions.

Voted against the bipartisan 2024 Senate border supplemental, publicly declaring it 'doesn't get the job done.' Demanded a 1,000-encounter-per-day enforcement threshold (versus the bill's 5,000), mandatory detention of illegal aliens instead of catch-and-release, and organized Republican senators to require a 72-hour public review period before any vote — insisting on hard enforcement over status quo.

profile.confidence_note: US House member bulk-scored via party-default archetype (maga_conservative_r). Per RESOLUTE Citizen methodology, silence on a major v4.0 issue is itself signal — a House member who has not publicly opposed a major issue is scored False on the affirmative-action question. Individual evidence review is pending; when specific votes/statements get promoted via apply-claims.py, this confidence will upgrade to archetype_curated. Run: rescore-house-by-party.py (2026-05-18). 2026-05-18 evidence: applied Iran war powers vote (H.Con.Res.38, 3/5/2026) — voted NAY per roll call source. 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on Laken Riley Act (S.5/H.R.29, signed 1/29/2025). 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (H.R.21/S.6, Jan 2025). 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on SAVE Act citizenship verification for voter registration (H.R.22, 4/10/2025). 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on NDAA FY26 (HR5371/S1071, 12/10/2025) — $900B defense + $400M Ukraine + Europe troop limits. 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (H.R.28/S.9, Jan/Mar 2025). 2026-05-18 evidence: voted YEA on Protect Children's Innocence Act (H.R.3492, 12/17/2025) — federal criminal ban on gender-affirming care for minors. · 2026-05-20 — Default-flagged as incumbent_seeking_reelection (loose-match cleanup pass).

candidates.name: Brinker Harding
candidates.office: U.S. Representative NE-02 (2026 R Nominee · Bacon-seat open · Trump-endorsed)
candidates.notes: Omaha city councilman. Ran unopposed in 2026 R primary for NE-02 — the "blue dot" district Kamala Harris won in 2024. Trump-endorsed. Will face Denise Powell (D) in November.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-19 — ingested for 2026 open House seat (NE-2). Archetype: maga_conservative_r.

candidates.name: Denise Powell
candidates.office: U.S. Representative NE-02 (2026 D Nominee · Bacon-seat open)
candidates.notes: Political organizer. Won 2026 D primary by ~2 points over state Sen. John Cavanaugh in the "blue dot" district. Will face Brinker Harding (R).

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-19 — ingested for 2026 open House seat (NE-2). Archetype: progressive_d.

candidates.name: Jim Pillen
candidates.office: Governor of Nebraska (R incumbent · 2026 reelection · hog farmer)
candidates.notes: R incumbent Governor of Nebraska (since 2023). Hog farmer, former NU Board of Regents. Seeking 2026 reelection.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-20 — 2026 NE Governor race. Archetype: establishment_r.

candidates.name: Dan Osborn
candidates.office: U.S. Senate Nebraska (2026 I Candidate · 2024 I nominee · union mechanic)
candidates.notes: Steamfitters union mechanic. 2024 Independent nominee for NE U.S. Senate (lost narrowly to Fischer in surprise close race). Likely 2026 I candidate for the special-election cycle Ricketts seat. Cross-pressure populist.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-20 — 2026 NE U.S. Senate challenger. Archetype: populist_right.
